mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for equity valuations and lowers corporate borrowing costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 10 percent, labor market stabilization signals reduced recession severity and supports consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in greater than 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses below the 10.0% threshold, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand recovery, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in greater than 60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with a p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-crisis liquidity injection regime reflects rapid supply chain normalization and inventory restocking, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day returns in greater than 55% of observations during post-crisis periods, or the mechanism fails to predict returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and corporate revenue projections, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 45% of months with payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 0.5 percent triggers expectations of central bank policy normalization and higher discount rates, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 70% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.5% monthly threshold, or the signal shows no predictive power when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP growth and declining unemployment rates confirm a synchronized economic expansion that lifts corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in greater than 60% of quarters, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production surge above 1.0 percent month-over-month signals rapid manufacturing recovery and capacity utilization gains, which immediately lifts equity prices and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day returns in greater than 65% of triggered months, or the predictive accuracy drops below random chance during periods of monetary tightening.
